summary: Ukrainian sources report increased drone activity across multiple northern and central oblasts, while Russian forces claim a successful strike on the Russian Navy corvette Boikiy in Kronstadt. Alerts highlight a high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk and Voronezh regions. Additional operational movements include the relocation of a Tu‑95MS bomber to Engels and ongoing reconnaissance missions in Zhytomyr and Sumy.
key_points: Ukrainian reconnaissance continues in Zhytomyr and Sumy regions (Source: @eRadarrua/95344).
New drone incursions reported in Chernihiv (3 near Snovsk) and Kharkiv (near Bohodukhiv) (Source: @eRadarrua/95343, @kpszsu/63909).
Satellite imagery shows Russian Navy corvette Boikiy damaged by fire in Kronstadt, Saint Petersburg (Source: @UkraineNow/81664).
High threat alerts issued for Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk and Voronezh oblasts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67473, @mon1tor_ua/67475).
13 Shahed drones reported heading toward Fountainka/Odesa (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67482).
A Tu‑95MS bomber relocated from Ukrainianka airfield to Engels, to be equipped with cruise missiles (Source: @eRadarrua/95327).
Russian claim of UAV "Geranium" striking the Nikopol power plant in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @tass_agency/379031).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian reconnaissance continues in Zhytomyr and Sumy while drone activity rises across Chernihiv, Kharkiv, and Dnipropetrovsk. Russian sources report damage to the corvette Boikiy in Kronstadt and claim a UAV strike on the Nikopol power plant. Alerts warn of imminent Iskander‑M missile launches from Bryansk and Voronezh, and a Tu‑95MS bomber is being repositioned to Engels with cruise missiles.

points: Ukrainian operatives are conducting reconnaissance missions in Zhytomyr and Sumy, specifically near Nedryhailiv, indicating ongoing intelligence gathering in the north‑west (Source: @eRadarrua/95344).

Ukrainian forces request equipment for the 112th Brigade on the Kupiansk direction, including a pump‑action shotgun for drone destruction, reflecting a need to counter aerial threats (Source: @eRadarrua/95353).
Ukrainian volunteers report a new reactive UAV entering northern Chernihiv heading toward Snovsk/Koryukivka, showing continued UAV deployment in the north (Source: @eRadarrua/95300).
Ukrainian sources claim a UAV "Geranium" struck the Nikopol power plant in Dnipropetrovsk region, suggesting offensive UAV use against critical infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/379031).

Russian Navy reports the corvette Boikiy (project 20380) was hit and set on fire in the port of Kronstadt, Saint Petersburg, indicating a successful strike on a naval asset (Source: @UkraineNow/81664).
Russia warns of a high threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk Oblast, highlighting potential long‑range strike capabilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67473).
A similar missile threat is noted from Voronezh Oblast, expanding the potential launch zones for Iskander‑M missiles (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67475).

Russia reports the relocation of a Tu‑95MS strategic bomber from Ukrainianka airfield to Engels, with plans to arm it with cruise missiles, indicating strategic bomber redeployment (Source: @eRadarrua/95327).
Ukrainian reconnaissance in Sumy (near Nedryhailiv) suggests monitoring of Russian movements on the eastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/95344).
Ukrainian UAV activity reported near Bohodukhiv in Kharkiv region, indicating pressure on Russian positions in the east (Source: @kpszsu/63909).
Russian claim of a UAV strike on the Nikopol power plant in Dnipropetrovsk region points to continued aerial attacks on eastern infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/379031).
Multiple Shahed drones reported heading toward Fountainka/Odesa, showing a northern approach to the southern coast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67482).
Ukrainian UAVs observed in Chernihiv region, with three near Snovsk and additional reactive UAVs heading toward Snovsk/Koryukivka (Source: @eRadarrua/95343, @eRadarrua/95300).
Drone sightings in northern Chernihiv (reactive Shahed) targeting Koriyivka and Mena indicate heightened aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/63889).
Thirteen Shahed drones are reported moving toward Fountainka/Odesa, suggesting a potential attack vector on the Black Sea coast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67482).
Ukrainian requests for equipment for the 112th Brigade on the Kupiansk direction highlight logistical needs on the southern front (Source: @eRadarrua/95353).
Ukrainian UAV "Geranium" allegedly struck the Nikopol power plant, demonstrating UAV strike capability against Russian energy assets (Source: @tass_agency/379031).
Russian alerts of Iskander‑M missile launches from Bryansk and Voronezh underscore the threat of high‑speed ballistic missiles in the air domain (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67473, @mon1tor_ua/67475).
Reports of Shahed drone formations heading toward Odesa indicate ongoing swarm attacks on Ukrainian air defenses (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67482).

key_developments: Ukrainian reconnaissance continues in Zhytomyr and Sumy, focusing on Nedryhailiv (Source: @eRadarrua/95344).
Three new Ukrainian drones were observed in Chernihiv near Snovsk (Source: @eRadarrua/95343).
Satellite images confirm the Russian corvette Boikiy was hit and set ablaze in Kronstadt, Saint Petersburg (Source: @UkraineNow/81664).
High threat alerts issued for Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk Oblast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67473).
Similar Iskander‑M launch threat reported from Voronezh Oblast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67475).
Thirteen Shahed drones are reported heading toward Fountainka/Odesa (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67482).
A Tu‑95MS bomber was relocated from Ukrainianka to Engels and will be equipped with cruise missiles (Source: @eRadarrua/95327).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces are intensifying reconnaissance and UAV operations across the northern and eastern fronts, targeting Russian logistics and infrastructure. Russia's response includes high‑profile naval damage claims and heightened missile launch alerts from western oblasts, indicating a potential escalation in long‑range strike capability. The relocation of a strategic bomber to Engels suggests Russia is bolstering its long‑range strike posture.
sections.regional_response: Western partners continue to discuss increased sanctions and aid, while regional actors such as Hungary signal political support for Ukraine's EU aspirations. No direct military assistance is detailed in the sources, but diplomatic statements reflect ongoing international engagement.
sections.intelligence_notes: The convergence of UAV activity, missile launch threats, and strategic bomber movements points to a multi‑domain escalation. Monitoring of Iskander‑M launch sites and UAV flight paths will be critical for early warning. Ukrainian requests for anti‑drone equipment highlight a growing need for counter‑UAV capabilities on the front lines.
